---
title: "MLCore Library"
description: "Non-Qt machine learning library providing algorithms on core data types"
---

## Overview

`MLCore` is a static C++ library (`src/MLCore/`) that provides machine learning algorithms operating directly on WhiskerToolbox core data types. It has **zero Qt dependency** and is fully testable in isolation.

The library provides:

- **Supervised classification**: Random Forest, Naive Bayes, Logistic Regression
- **Unsupervised clustering**: K-Means, DBSCAN, Gaussian Mixture Model
- **End-to-end pipelines** that compose validation, conversion, training/fitting, prediction, metrics, and output writing into single entry points
- **Feature validation and conversion** from `TensorData` to armadillo matrices
- **Label assembly** from multiple sources (intervals, entity groups, data-entity groups)
- **Prediction output** following the putative group pattern

## Architecture

```
MLCore (static, no Qt)
├── features/          # TensorData validation, conversion, label assembly
├── metrics/           # Binary & multi-class classification metrics
├── preprocessing/     # Class balancing (subsample / oversample)
├── models/            # Abstract interface + concrete implementations
│   ├── supervised/    # Random Forest, Naive Bayes, Logistic Regression
│   └── unsupervised/  # K-Means, DBSCAN, Gaussian Mixture
├── output/            # PredictionWriter — putative group pattern output
├── entity_utils/      # Bulk TimeEntity registration & conversion
└── pipelines/         # ClassificationPipeline, ClusteringPipeline
```

### Dependency Graph

```
MLCore
├── DataManager          # Data storage & retrieval
│   ├── TensorData       # Feature matrices
│   ├── AnalogTimeSeries # Probability output
│   └── DigitalTimeSeries# Interval output
├── Entity               # EntityRegistry & EntityGroupManager
│   ├── EntityRegistry
│   └── EntityGroupManager
├── MLPack (header-only) # ML algorithms
└── Armadillo            # Linear algebra backend
```

## Key Design Decisions

### TensorData as Feature Matrix

Feature assembly is **not** part of MLCore. Users build feature tensors using the TensorDesigner/TensorColumnBuilders infrastructure. MLCore validates, converts (`float→double`, NaN handling, z-score normalization), and consumes the result via `FeatureConverter`.

### Pimpl Pattern for Model Isolation

All model operations (Random Forest, K-Means, etc.) use the pimpl idiom to isolate mlpack headers from consumers. This keeps compile times reasonable and prevents mlpack's transitive includes from leaking.

### Putative Group Output Pattern

Predictions never overwrite user labels. Output goes to groups prefixed with the configured prefix (e.g., `"Predicted:Contact"`, `"Cluster:0"`). This follows the pattern established by `LineKalmanGrouping` elsewhere in the project.

### Three Label Modes

Labels can come from:

1. **DigitalIntervalSeries** — binary labeling (inside=positive, outside=negative)
2. **TimeEntity groups** — multi-class from entity group membership at each frame
3. **Data-entity groups** — multi-class from data object group membership

See [Label Assembly](label_assembly.qmd) for details.

## Contents

- [Model Interface & Registry](models.qmd) — `MLModelOperation`, `MLModelRegistry`, task types, parameters
- [Feature Processing](features.qmd) — validation, conversion, label assembly
- [Pipelines](pipelines.qmd) — `ClassificationPipeline` and `ClusteringPipeline`
- [Label Assembly](label_assembly.qmd) — three labeling modes in detail
- [Output & Entity Utils](output.qmd) — `PredictionWriter`, `TimeEntityUtils`

## Built-in Models

| Model | Task Type | Key Parameters | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Random Forest | MultiClassClassification | `num_trees`, `minimum_leaf_size`, `maximum_depth` | Probability output via vote fraction |
| Naive Bayes | MultiClassClassification | `epsilon` (variance smoothing) | Fast, works well with independent features |
| Logistic Regression | BinaryClassification | `lambda` (L2), `max_iterations` | Well-calibrated probabilities via sigmoid |
| K-Means | Clustering | `k`, `max_iterations`, `seed` | Centroid-based assignment for new data |
| DBSCAN | Clustering | `epsilon`, `min_points` | Noise points labeled `SIZE_MAX` |
| Gaussian Mixture | Clustering | `k`, `max_iterations`, `seed` | Soft assignments via `clusterProbabilities()` |

## Test Coverage

285 test cases with 2899 assertions across 16 test files in `tests/MLCore/`. Key test areas:

| Area | Files | Coverage |
|---|---|---|
| Model operations | 6 model test files | Training, prediction, probabilities, save/load, multi-class |
| Pipelines | `ClassificationPipeline.test.cpp`, `ClusteringPipeline.test.cpp` | End-to-end with all models, error handling, balancing |
| Features | `FeatureValidator.test.cpp`, `FeatureConverter.test.cpp` | RowDescriptor compatibility, NaN dropping, z-score |
| Labels | `LabelAssembler.test.cpp` | All three labeling modes |
| Metrics | `ClassificationMetrics.test.cpp` | Binary/multi-class metrics, formatting |
| Preprocessing | `ClassBalancing.test.cpp` | Subsample/oversample, deterministic seeding |
| Output | `PredictionWriter.test.cpp` | Interval output, probability series, putative groups |
| Entity utils | `TimeEntityUtils.test.cpp` | Bulk registration, interval ↔ entity round-trip |
